text.title: Ideation Methods: SCAMPER, Mind Mapping, Starbursting Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding ideation-methods. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: SCAMPER MUST have a concrete existing subject. Applying SCAMPER to a vacuum produces nonsense.
rule.rationale: SCAMPER's lenses operate on the subject's attributes; no attributes, no operation.

rule.statement: SCAMPER MUST apply all 7 lenses (Substitute, Combine, Adapt, Modify, Put to other use, Eliminate, Reverse) — skipping lenses produces an under-explored variant set.
rule.rationale: Each lens surfaces a distinct variant class; partial application leaves the most novel ones uncovered.

rule.statement: Mind Map MUST have at least 3 levels of depth from the central node. Single-level branching is a list, not a map.
rule.rationale: Depth surfaces second- and third-order connections that flat lists miss.

rule.statement: Starbursting MUST cover all 6 question types (Who, What, Where, When, Why, How) with ≥3 questions each before any answer is attempted.
rule.rationale: Skipping a W/H type leaves a blind spot; the lazy default is to skip 'Why' which is the highest-signal one.

rule.statement: Output MUST be a structured worksheet (SCAMPER table, Mind Map node tree, or Starbursting matrix), not a raw list.
rule.rationale: Worksheets force completeness checks and produce inputs that downstream PMs can act on.
